original_abstract: This research explores the strategies employed by Islamic Religious Education (PAI) teachers at SDN 2 Kesumadadi to cultivate religious character in elementary school students. The study is motivated by the need to instill spiritual values at an early age and addresses specific challenges at the school, such as students' limited proficiency in reading the Quran. Using a qualitative field research approach, data were gathered through in-depth interviews, field observations, and documentation analysis. The findings reveal that the primary strategy utilized is the systematic habituation of reciting the Asma' al-Husna (the 99 Names of Allah). Although this practice is integrated into religious education lessons rather than daily morning routines, it serves as a vital tool for internalizing divine attributes and improving students' spiritual literacy. To support this habituation, teachers employ multifaceted approaches including modeling exemplary behavior, providing continuous mentoring, offering positive reinforcement through appreciation, and enforcing consistent discipline. The results indicate that this strategy not only enhances students' religious knowledge and memorization but also positively impacts their broader character development, fostering discipline, polite communication, and increased enthusiasm for learning. This study highlights the importance of consistent school-based religious routines in shaping the moral and spiritual foundation of primary-level students.
humanized_abstract: This study aims to investigate the strategy used by teachers of Islamic Religious Education (PAI) at SDN 2 Kesumadadi to cultivate religious character of elementary school students. This study is motivated by the necessity of building religious character from an early age, and it aims to solve the problems faced by the school, namely the low ability of elementary school students in reading the Quran. This study uses qualitative field research method with data collection through depth interviews, field observation, and documentation analysis. The results of the study show that the strategy used by PAI teachers is by habituating students in reading Asma' al-Husna. Although this habituation is carried out in the context of Islamic Religious Education lessons (PAI) and is not performed as a daily morning routine, it is very effective in nurturing the students' religious character. The teacher uses various strategies to habituate students in reading Asma' al-Husna, namely by illustrating and applying the norms of religion, by giving guidance continuously, by giving appreciation to students, and through discipline in order to maintain the students' behaviour. The result shows that the strategy used by the teachers to cultivate religious character of students through habituation of reading Asma' al-Husna can contribute to increasing the students' religious knowledge and ability to memorise and to cultivate the students' overall character, namely discipline, good communication skill, and interest in learning. This study is expected to be useful in understanding the importance of building religious character at school by arranging the students' religious activities systematically so as to establish a strong religious character for elementary school students.
